# Transcrição: 12-How to Get Ads Approved

- **Fonte:** 12-How to Get Ads Approved
- **Duração:** 02:37
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:37]

So this step is really really important. Before we go live with our ads and with the campaigns that we're running, we need to make sure that the client approves the ads. So what we do is we keep it all in this Google Sheet and we create this Google Sheet initially when we set up our campaigns with the client and each time that we need to update ad copy or ad creative, we'll add a new tab here. So we'll just click add or we'll duplicate this one. So we'll duplicate it and then call it September 21st. Say that's the next time that we need them to approve these ads. So essentially each time it's easy for the client to go into this Google Doc and we're able to see exactly if they've approved it or if they haven't and they're able to go back as well. So they're able to actually monitor the comments, the likes, the shares, they're able to monitor the engagement and the community management side of things by going back to this spreadsheet as well. So it makes it really, really easy for them. So in order to do that, we basically segmented out based on persona and we're going to have a different line for each of the different ads. So initially we're going to go to the ads manager. We need to pull up, we just need to go to the ads section and go to edit and then this little box right here, you just click on it and go to see post, Facebook post with comments. I'm going to open that in a new tab. So that's going to open up and then this URL at the very top, that's the URL that we're going to copy and paste and this is just showing the live ad here and then you're able to go back here and paste it in there. So now when a client clicks on this, they're able to see the comments, they're able to see the likes, the shares and all of the social proof in the ad as well. Now it's also really important that you do an internal check to make sure that everything looks good before you fire it off to the client. So here you just want to make sure that you do that internal check and if you write yes or no, that's essentially what you want to do here. So if you're the only person managing these ads and if you're the one creating them, then just take a day or take a couple of hours and come back with a fresh mind and look at them, make sure there's no obvious spelling mistakes or anything like that. If you have a team that you're working with, you just want to make sure that your team reviews the ads. What's also great here is that your client can leave comments directly related to the ad that you're talking about. So it kind of avoids the back and forth in an email or in a message or even on the phone, you can see exactly which one they're referring to and you can see their exact comments on the ads.
